subtitle: no heroes — only tropes, and the parables underneath
author: Stephen R. Donaldson
ring: a science-fiction reforging of Wagner's Ring: Warden Dios as the one-eyed god who pays, Holt Fasner as the hoarding dragon, Morn as the will that chooses.
note: The Gap Cycle sorted not by heroism — there is none — but by trope and parable, in the manner of the muster roll. Every member carries a .agent (who you are · what you do · where you belong). roster.json is the source of truth. Characters by Stephen R. Donaldson, referenced as inspiration only; the cycle's darkest material is treated here as archetype, not detail.

classes.label: The God Who Pays
classes.spec: the long game · self spent on a bet

classes.label: The Honest Blade
classes.spec: fidelity kept when all else bends

members.name: Holt Fasner
members.specialty: CEO of the United Mining Companies — the Dragon in his warren, chasing immortality through other people's bodies. The hoard that eats the world to live one more day. (Wagner's Fafner, grown corporate.)

members.name: Warden Dios
members.specialty: Director of the UMCP — the one-eyed man who spends his own name, his honor, and his soul on a decades-long bet that humanity, given the truth, will choose well. The god who pays the price himself. (Wotan.)

members.name: Morn Hyland
members.specialty: UMCP ensign; the still, burning center of the cycle. Used by every power that touches her — and who wrenches her own agency back from all of them. The will to choose, reforged from violation. (Brünnhilde.)

members.name: Angus Thermopyle
members.specialty: Illegal miner, brutal and broken; the abused who became the abuser; welded into a cyborg slave by a zone implant. The cycle's deepest, hardest question made flesh: whether even he can find a sliver of grace.

members.name: Nick Succorso
members.specialty: Pirate captain whose scars flush when he kills; charm worn like a knife; vanity that mistakes itself for destiny — and breaks when the universe declines to agree.

members.name: Sorus Chatelaine
members.specialty: Captain of Soar; trader with the Amnion; a predator who is herself caged — cruelty as the shape of her own captivity.

members.name: Hashi Lebwohl
members.specialty: UMCP Data Acquisition; amoral genius who spins manipulation as fine art; never lies, never says it plain; serves an order only he can see.

members.name: Min Donner
members.specialty: UMCP Enforcement Division director; the hard, honest cop; keeps faith and her sidearm when everyone above her bends. The one who will not look away. (The Valkyrie who stays true.)

members.name: The Amnion
members.specialty: Alien gene-imperium; trades in mutagens; offers transformation as the annihilation of the self. The consumption that wears your face afterward and calls it cooperation.

members.name: Vector Shaheed
members.specialty: Geneticist turned ship's mechanic; calm hands, old guilt; atones by building the one thing that can undo the Amnion mutagen — and broadcasting the cure to everyone, free. 'Just doing my job.'

members.name: Davies Hyland
members.specialty: Morn's son, force-grown to a man in hours and given her memories; a child made, not born, who must choose what to do with a self he was handed.

members.name: Mikka, Ciro & Sib
members.specialty: The crew of Trumpet — ordinary people crushed and then ennobled by the machine; the ones the parable is finally about.

members.name: No Heroes Here
members.specialty: The Gap Cycle keeps no heroes. It keeps a dragon's greed, a god who pays, a monster who reaches for grace, a survivor who chooses anyway — and asks whether the will to choose, freely, is the only freedom there is. The Ring reforged: power corrupts, transformation costs the self, and redemption is possible only at a price no hero would pay. That is the parable. Witness it.
